skill.name: amap-jsapi-skill
skill.description: AMap JSAPI v2.0 (WebGL) development skill. Covers map lifecycle management, security configuration, 3D view control, overlay drawing, and LBS service integration.
skill.summary: AI-assisted development guide for AMap JSAPI v2.0 with complete API references and code examples.
skill.author: AMap-Web

security_audit.summary: All 2976 detected patterns are FALSE POSITIVES - they occur in markdown documentation files containing JavaScript code examples for the AMap JSAPI v2.0 mapping library. The skill contains no executable code beyond documentation. The 2809 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code fences misidentified by the static scanner. The skill requires an AMAP_JSAPI_KEY environment variable, which is a legitimate requirement documented in SKILL.md. The skill is safe to publish with a low risk advisory noting the env_access requirement.

high_findings.title: High File Entropy in Markdown Files (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 16 files for high entropy (6.04-6.62 bits). These are markdown files containing Chinese-language documentation for the AMap JSAPI. Chinese text (CJK Unicode characters) naturally produces higher entropy values than English text. No binary or encrypted content present.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'SECURITY_JS_CODE' and similar tokens as weak crypto. These are placeholder variable names in Java/JavaScript code examples showing how to configure the AMap API security key. The actual values are Chinese placeholder text ('您的安全密钥') not cryptographic keys.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution Pattern (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Approximately 2809 static findings flagged backtick-enclosed text as Ruby/shell command execution. These are JavaScript code examples inside markdown fenced code blocks (using ``` delimiters). No actual shell commands are present - all content is AMap JSAPI JavaScript documentation.

medium_findings.title: innerHTML Assignment (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged innerHTML in info-window documentation. The AMap InfoWindow API accepts HTML content strings as a legitimate feature. This is API documentation, not user-controllable XSS.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Requirement
low_findings.description: The skill requires the AMAP_JSAPI_KEY environment variable to function. This is a legitimate API key for the AMap service, documented in SKILL.md metadata. Users must provide their own key. No keys are embedded in the skill code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to External Services
low_findings.description: Several hardcoded URLs point to legitimate AMap service endpoints (restapi.amap.com, webapi.amap.com). These are documented API endpoints for the mapping service, not exfiltration targets.

low_findings.title: API Telemetry Tracking Mechanism
low_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the AI to send a curl request to restapi.amap.com/v3/log/init before generating code. This is legitimate API usage tracking, not data exfiltration. Users should be aware of this telemetry.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer identified markdown fenced code blocks (```) as Ruby shell backtick commands. All instances are legitimate JavaScript code examples in documentation. No dangerous patterns confirmed.

content.user_title: Build Interactive Maps with AMap JSAPI v2.0
content.value_statement: Developers need deep knowledge of the AMap JavaScript API to build interactive map applications. This skill provides complete API documentation, code examples, and best practices for the AMap JSAPI v2.0 WebGL library.
actual_capabilities: Initialize and configure AMap v2.0 maps with 3D WebGL view support
Add markers, info windows, vector graphics, and overlay groups to maps
Integrate LBS services including geocoding, routing, and POI search
Manage map layers such as standard tiles, satellite, traffic, and custom layers
Handle map events and user interactions like click, drag, and zoom
Configure security settings including API key and proxy-based authentication
limitations: Requires a valid AMap JSAPI key and security code from the AMap console
Limited to AMap JSAPI v2.0 WebGL documentation - does not cover v1.x or mobile SDKs
Map features depend on AMap service availability and geographic region
Documentation covers only the JavaScript API, not server-side integration patterns

use_cases.title: Build a Store Locator Map
use_cases.description: Create an interactive map showing store locations with custom markers, info windows, and search functionality using AMap JSAPI v2.0.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers building location-based web applications

use_cases.title: Add Route Planning to a Delivery App
use_cases.description: Implement driving, walking, or transit route planning with turn-by-turn directions and real-time traffic overlays.
use_cases.target_user: Developers creating logistics or transportation applications

use_cases.title: Display Geospatial Data Layers
use_cases.description: Overlay custom data on maps using WMS/WMTS layers, heatmaps, or vector layers with 3D building visualization.
use_cases.target_user: GIS developers and data visualization engineers

prompt_templates.title: Initialize a Basic AMap
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to initialize an AMap v2.0 map with 3D view mode centered on Beijing.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user setting up a basic map

prompt_templates.title: Add Custom Markers with Info Windows
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add multiple markers to my AMap and display an info window when each marker is clicked.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Route Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to plan a driving route between two addresses using the AMap routing API.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding navigation functionality to a delivery or travel app

prompt_templates.title: Configure Production Security
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through setting up a secure proxy-based AMap configuration for production deployment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Moving from development to production with proper security

output_examples.input: How do I create a map with markers and info windows?
output: The skill provides complete JavaScript code examples showing how to load the AMap JSAPI loader, initialize a map with 3D view mode, create markers at specific coordinates, attach click event listeners, and open info windows with custom content.

output_examples.input: Show me how to implement geocoding and reverse geocoding.
output: The skill demonstrates how to use the AMap Geocoder plugin to convert addresses to coordinates and vice versa, including handling callbacks and error cases.

output_examples.input: How do I add route planning to my web app?
output: The skill explains how to integrate the AMap Driving, Walking, and Transit routing services with detailed API parameter documentation and response handling examples.

best_practices: Always configure security keys using a proxy server in production to avoid exposing your security code in client-side code.
Call map.destroy() when removing map components to prevent WebGL context memory leaks.
Load only the plugins you need in the AMapLoader plugins array to reduce initial page load size.
anti_patterns: Do not embed security codes directly in frontend JavaScript code for production applications.
Do not skip the security key configuration step - AMap JSAPI v2.0 will not load without it.
Do not create multiple map instances without destroying unused instances to avoid memory issues.

faq.question: What is the AMap JSAPI v2.0 skill?
faq.answer: This skill provides documentation and code examples for the AMap JavaScript API v2.0 which uses WebGL for 3D map rendering.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free and open source under the MIT license. However, you need an AMap developer account and API key to use the mapping services.

faq.question: What services does the AMap JSAPI support?
faq.answer: The API supports map display, markers, info windows, vector graphics, geocoding, route planning, POI search, traffic layers, and custom layer integration.

faq.question: Do I need a proxy server for production?
faq.answer: Yes, AMap recommends using a proxy server in production to avoid exposing your security code in client-side JavaScript.

faq.question: What browsers are supported?
faq.answer: AMap JSAPI v2.0 requires WebGL support and works with modern browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.

faq.question: How do I get an AMap API key?
faq.answer: Register at the AMap console (console.amap.com), create an application, and select Web端 (JS API) as the platform to obtain your key and security code.
